entries.description: Set 030 initialized: session-state v3 sessions ledger + terminology alignment (5 sessions). Proposal authored by/with GPT-5.4, strong-approved by Gemini Pro. Operator-locked decisions: complete-everywhere terminology unification, regex-first AI-fallback migration, on-activation detection trigger, Setting-up-your-project loading state.

Set 030 spec revised per GPT-5.4 implementation review (2026-05-17). Five changes applied: (1) final publish moved from S4 to S5 (after migration UX ships) - D14; (2) dual-write kept as operational steady state, stop-legacy deferred to future set gated on consumer v3-reader confirmation - D5 revision; (3) terminology drift resolved by updating proposal doc to use 'complete' instead of 'done' globally - D2 alignment; (4) lint rule narrowed to 'no application reader may read legacy fields except through approved compat helpers' - D13; (5) spec-title-extraction routing config registered in S1 instead of S5 - removes dependency risk. Added D15 (Set 029 ordering: both orders supported) and D16 (Lightweight ergonomics dry-run vs accessdb state file before publish).

Read existing schema doc, examples, proposal, session_state.py, types.ts, router-config.yaml, test_session_state_v2.py to map baseline state of v2 schema, existing test conventions, and extension wiring. Confirmed proposal terminology was already 95% aligned to 'complete'; identified two lingering 'done' references at lines 139 and 246 that need patching per D2.

Created ai_router/progress.py (~530 LOC) with ProgressView + SessionRecord dataclasses, SessionStateInvariantError exception, canonicalize_status alias map, extract_session_titles_from_spec regex helper, synthesize_v3_from_v2 read-time normalizer, get_progress + validate_invariants enforcing all 8 invariants. Pure functional (no filesystem coupling beyond the spec.md read in extract_session_titles_from_spec).

Created ai_router/tests/test_progress.py covering v3 happy paths (fresh/in-flight/between/complete/cancelled), each of the 8 invariant violations with rule-number assertions, v2 synthesis (in-flight, between, complete-consistent, complete-with-missing-fails-loud, alias canonicalization, spec-missing fallback, bool/float type confusion), title extraction (4 cases), canonicalizeStatus (8 parametric cases), and direct validator entry with top_status=None. 40 → 48 tests after Round A.

Created tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/utils/progress.ts (~440 LOC) as line-for-line TS mirror of progress.py. Updated tools/.../src/types.ts adding SessionStatus, SessionRecord, ProgressView, SessionStateV3 interfaces (kept legacy SessionState 'done'|'in-progress'|... type alias for the extension's bucketing layer until Session 3 migrates it). Created tools/.../test/suite/progress.test.ts mirroring the pytest suite shape (mocha tdd). 38 → 46 tests after Round A. tsc --noEmit clean.

Rewrote docs/session-state-schema.md from v2 to v3. New sections: 'v3 is canonical; v2 read support is permanent', 'Reader contract — every reader uses get_progress()', 'v3 schema shape', 'sessions[] — the canonical progress ledger', 'Dual-write legacy fields (Set 030 steady state)', 'Status — the canonical glossary' with the Set 030 terminology note retiring 'Done' from display labels, 'Derived values — the get_progress() view', the 8 invariants with explicit rule-2-tightened-to-contiguous-from-1 note, Lightweight one-field-flip worked example with every transition, v3 worked examples (not-started/mid-set/between-sessions/complete), tier expectations, v2 read compat path with default-to-not-started rule explanation, bucketing rules.

Rewrote docs/session-state-schema-example.json to v3 closed shape (schemaVersion: 3, sessions[] with 4 entries all complete, dual-write legacy triple still present). Rewrote docs/session-state-schema-example.md with v2-vs-v3 side-by-side narrative, lifecycle shapes (not-started/in-progress/complete), and regenerator workflow notes. Patched lingering 'done' references in docs/proposals/2026-05-17-...md at lines 139 and 246 to 'complete' per spec D2.

Registered spec-title-extraction task type in ai_router/router-config.yaml per spec D14 (removes Session 5 dependency risk). Added task_type_overrides entry pinning to gemini-flash; task_type_scores entry at 20 (low/mechanical band); per-model task_type_params with thinking off for cost predictability. Created ai_router/tests/test_spec_title_extraction_registered.py with 6 guard tests asserting the routing wiring stays correct (score in low band, override pins tier-1 model, per-model params present for all 6 pool models, pick_model resolves via override, NOT in always_route_task_types, NOT in auto_verify_task_types).

Ran full pytest suite (testpaths = ai_router/tests, -m 'not e2e'): 476 passed, 1 skipped, 8 e2e deselected. No regressions in existing suite from Session 1's additions. Confirmed test_dump_session_state_schema.py is broken pre-existing (import issue, predates Session 1).

Round A verification: gpt-5-4 via ai_router.route(task_type='session-verification'). Initial Round A attempt with ~2000 LOC bundle (Python+TS helpers + tests + types + config) hit 3-attempt read timeout. Retried with tighter scope (Python helper only, ~514 LOC) per memory feedback_split_large_verification_bundles. Cost: $0.275525, elapsed 213s, in=5138 out=17512 tokens, no escalation. Verifier returned REJECTED with 6 must-fix issues.

Applied all 6 must-fix issues from Round A: (1) removed synthesize_v3_from_v2 force-promote-on-complete (now defaults to not-started; rule 7 surfaces contradiction); (2) added strict-int filter (Python: type(v) is int; TS: Number.isInteger + typeof !== boolean) to reject bool/float in v2 currentSession/completedSessions[]; (3) tightened rule 2 to contiguous-from-1 per spec D12 (rejects [1, 3] and [2, 3]); (4) removed 'cancelled' from SESSION_STATUSES (top-level still accepted); (5) hoisted rule 8 above the top_status None-guard so it always fires; (6) changed unknown top-level status from rule 5 to rule 2 (shape/enum). Updated 8 pytest cases + 8 mocha cases for new behavior. Updated schema.md, example.md to document tightened invariants and fail-loud synthesizer.

Re-ran full pytest suite after Round A fixes: 484 passed, 1 skipped, 8 e2e deselected (+8 vs pre-Session-1). Mocha progress.test.ts: 46/46. TypeScript tsc --noEmit: clean. No regressions in any pre-existing tests.

Registered Session 2 start via python -m ai_router.start_session. Snapshot flipped to currentSession=2, completedSessions=[1] preserved from Session 1 close-out. work_started event #2 appended to session-events.jsonl.

Bumped SCHEMA_VERSION 2->3. Added 5 v3 helpers to session_state.py (_existing_sessions_records, _spec_titles_for_set, _build_sessions_array, _derive_legacy_fields, _validate_sessions_or_raise). Re-exported SessionStateInvariantError + 3 session-status constants + helpers from progress.py so writers raise the same exception class the read-side validators use. Updated _migrate_v1_to_v2_inplace gate to literal '>= 2' so v2 files pass through on read (v3 promotion is the writer's job).

register_session_start rewritten to dual-write per spec D5: builds v3 sessions[] via _build_sessions_array, validates via _validate_sessions_or_raise BEFORE any file write or event emission (per spec D6 + Round-A fix #4), then derives the legacy triple via _derive_legacy_fields. effective_total fallback: caller-supplied -> existing state -> max(spec_titles, completed, session_number). Includes Round-A fix #1 fail-loud rejection of session_number > effective_total and prior completed > effective_total.

_flip_state_to_closed rewritten to dual-write. Resolves totalSessions through the fallback chain; raises SessionStateInvariantError if still 0 (Round-A fix #3). Splits forced (incident-recovery promotes 1..total to complete) from natural close (uses new_completed as-is; validator catches gaps - Round-A fix #2). Always builds v3 sessions[], always validates before write.

_not_started_payload now includes a v3 sessions[] array when totalSessions is known from spec.md (all entries not-started). Per rule 1, sessions[] is omitted only when totalSessions is unknown ('any set with a known plan'). _backfill_payload promotes the array's per-session statuses to match the inferred top-status: change-log present -> all complete; activity-log only -> session 1 in-progress (conservative); neither -> all not-started. All scaffolding paths run _validate_sessions_or_raise.

Updated test_session_state_v2.py for v3 dual-write semantics: schemaVersion assertions bumped 2->3; test_writes_v3_with_lifecycle_state asserts sessions[] present with session 1 in-progress + others not-started + derived legacy triple; test_writes_closed_lifecycle_state restructured to close sessions 1 and 2 naturally (without force=True) so the final state passes invariants; test_mark_complete_rewrites_v1_as_current_schema updated to assert forced=True now promotes all 5 sessions to complete (incident-recovery semantic) + forceClosed=True marker.

Updated 3 downstream tests for v3 currentSession semantics (strictly the in-progress session's number, or None when no session in-flight - replaces v2's ambiguous 'in-flight OR most-recently-closed'): test_happy_3session.test_happy_3session_full_cycle (currentSession is None after every close); test_force_close_path.test_force_close_nonfinal_session (completedSessions=[1,2,3] under forced incident-recovery, not [1,2]); test_close_session_snapshot_flip.test_close_session_multi_session_set_clean (currentSession is None after final close).

Created ai_router/tests/test_session_state_v3.py (~720 LOC). 37 mocha-style test cases covering: SCHEMA_VERSION; _build_sessions_array unit tests (status assignment, title carry-forward, generic fallback, rejection of invalid total); _derive_legacy_fields unit tests; register_session_start v3 dual-write (sessions[] + legacy triple, title carry-forward across spec.md mutation, dual-write parity at session 2 start, idempotent work_started, totalSessions backfill); mark_session_complete v3 dual-write (mid-set keeps SET in-progress, final close flips to complete, forced promotes all); scaffolding writes v3 (synthesize_not_started_state, ensure_session_state_file, backfill_session_state_files); writer-rejects-out-of-range (Round-A fix coverage); flip-requires-totalSessions; natural-close-does-not-promote-all; SessionStateInvariantError re-export identity.

Ran full pytest suite after writer changes + test updates: 522 passed, 1 skipped, 8 e2e deselected (+38 vs pre-Session-2). 3 downstream failures identified and fixed inline (currentSession-after-close semantic shift). TypeScript tsc --noEmit clean (no TS-side code changed; Session 3 owns the reader migration).

Round A verification: gpt-5-4 via ai_router.route(task_type='session-verification'). Focused slice of session_state.py (~770 LOC across 7 ranges per memory feedback_split_large_verification_bundles). Cost: $0.266495, elapsed 242s, in=13058 out=15590 tokens, no escalation. Verifier returned REJECTED with 4 must-fix issues: (1) silent truncation of out-of-range session numbers; (2) _flip_state_to_closed promotes all on natural close (not just forced); (3) unvalidated legacy-only fallback write when totalSessions unknown; (4) work_started event emitted before invariant validation.

Applied all 4 must-fix issues from Round A: (1) _build_sessions_array now raises rule 2 if in_progress_number or any completed_numbers fall outside [1, total]; register_session_start raises rule 2 if session_number > effective_total or max(prior_completed) > effective_total; (2) _flip_state_to_closed now splits forced (promote 1..total to complete) from natural close (completed_for_array = new_completed; validator surfaces any gap) at lines 807-843; (3) removed legacy-only fallback write; if totalSessions can't be resolved through fallbacks, raise rule 1 at lines 790-797; (4) reordered register_session_start: build sessions[] -> validate -> emit work_started -> write snapshot (was: emit -> build -> validate -> write). Added 7 regression tests covering each fix in test_session_state_v3.py.

Re-ran full pytest suite after Round A fixes: 529 passed, 1 skipped, 8 e2e deselected (+7 vs pre-Round-A from new regression tests). TypeScript tsc --noEmit clean. No regressions in any pre-existing tests.

Round B verification: gpt-5-4 via ai_router.route(task_type='session-verification'). Same slice strategy as Round A; updated line ranges for the post-fix file (~870 LOC across 7 ranges). Cost: $0.193638, elapsed 151s, in=13249 out=10701 tokens, no escalation. Verifier returned VERIFIED with explicit line-by-line confirmation for each of the 4 Round A issues plus 'no new contradiction' assessment per fix.

Registered Session 3 start via python -m ai_router.start_session. Snapshot now currentSession=3, completedSessions=[1,2]. sessions[] from Session 2's writer is preserved with sessions[2].status flipped from complete to in-progress (actually, Session 3 in-progress; sessions 1,2 stayed complete). work_started event #3 appended to session-events.jsonl. Set 030 progress: 2/5 (40%) starting Session 3.

Grep audit for direct legacy-field reads across both codebases. Python: 33 files matched; production readers identified in close_session.py, gate_checks.py, start_session.py, __init__.py (plus the writer-allowed session_state.py and the carrier-field reader session_log.py). TypeScript: 20 files matched; production readers identified in fileSystem.ts (readSessionSets + isMidSetComplete) and SessionSetsProvider.ts (predicate + label). Carve-outs identified per D13: progress.py/progress.ts (helpers), session_state.py/sessionState.ts (writers), close_session._run_repair (v2-compat repair walk).

Added read_progress(state, spec_md_path) to ai_router/progress.py as the canonical reader entry point per D13. Branches v3 (state.sessions present) vs v2 (synthesize first) internally. Mirror readProgress(state, specMdPath) added to tools/.../utils/progress.ts. Both raise SessionStateInvariantError on invariant violation; application readers wrapping in try/catch get defensive degradation. Added 5 pytest cases + 5 mocha cases covering the wrapper.

Migrated 4 Python reader sites: gate_checks.py three gates (check_activity_log_entry, check_next_orchestrator_present, check_change_log_fresh) via two new helpers (_read_progress_or_none + _session_in_focus); start_session.py preflight (current_in_flight + total_sessions derivation); close_session.py _peek_session_number; __init__.py print_session_set_status cost reporter. close_session._run_repair retains 3 inline # noqa: D13 markers (file-level allowlist; repair walks are v2-compat by definition). __init__.py line 898 keeps # noqa: D13 for the activity-log carrier-field read (different artifact's schema).

Renamed SessionState union literal 'done' -> 'complete' across the TypeScript codebase per spec D3. Touched: types.ts (the union), fileSystem.ts (STATE_RANK + isMidSetComplete return), SessionSetsProvider.ts (ICON_FILES key + group label 'Done' -> 'Complete' + progressText annotation + groupKey checks), troubleshoot.ts (user-visible help string), e2eHarness.ts (key type), 4 e2e test files (childrenOfGroup arg), 2 unit test files (state fixtures), 5 readSessionSets test assertions. tsc --noEmit clean after the cascade.

Migrated tools/.../utils/fileSystem.ts: isMidSetComplete collapsed from the Set 022 + Set 023 multi-signal predicate to a single v3 invariant probe (try readProgress; rule-N violation -> mid-set). v2-compat ledger-merge pre-processing populates completedSessions[] from session-events.jsonl when missing (preserves pre-Set-022 snapshots). readSessionSets state-file block migrated through readProgress; liveSession.currentSession/completedSessions/totalSessions/sessionsCompleted all derive from the v3 view. New helper readClosedSessionsFromLedger replaces the inlined version. SessionSetsProvider.ts isCurrentSessionInFlight simplified to a null-check on the v3 currentSession field (no longer needs the v2 'currentSession not in completedSessions[]' predicate).

Updated tools/.../utils/sessionState.ts lazy-synth writers to mirror Session 2's Python writer changes. SCHEMA_VERSION bumped 2->3. New buildSessions(total, topStatus) helper produces v3 sessions[] for the inferred top-status. notStartedPayload and backfillPayload now emit the v3 dual-write shape (sessions[] + derived legacy triple) when spec.md declares totalSessions. Per rule 1, sessions[] is omitted when totalSessions is unknown - in that case the original (pre-fix) backfill code still escalated to status=complete which would fail readProgress; this is the Round A finding and is fixed in step 12 below.

Added two D13 lint tests. ai_router/tests/test_no_legacy_field_reads.py: pytest grep guard targeting dict-access patterns (.get('X'), ['X']); allowlist progress.py + session_state.py + session_log.py + close_session.py (file-level: _run_repair is v2-compat) + tests/ + scripts/ + lines with '# noqa: D13'; sanity-check test confirms the scanner walks the tree. tools/.../test/suite/noLegacyFieldReads.test.ts: Mocha equivalent targeting raw state-dict access patterns (sd.X, state.X, etc.) so downstream SessionSet/ProgressView reads aren't false-positives; allowlist mirrors the Python side plus providers/SessionSetsProvider.ts (reads SessionSet model, not raw state).

Updated 24 test cases for v3 semantics: e2e treeProvider-{force,happy,multiset,worktree}.test.ts (Done -> Complete strings, force-closed bucket change, session-1-in-flight annotation now fires); fileSystem.test.ts (state='done' -> 'complete', v2 fixtures need completedSessions[] for status=complete to validate, lazy-synth tests need spec totalSessions); cancelTreeView.test.ts (setupSet helper now emits v3-shape snapshots); sessionSetsProvider.test.ts (rewrote isCurrentSessionInFlight tests around v3 semantic - currentSession=null between sessions vs in-progress); fleshed out tests for v3 currentSession=null after each close. All test fixtures consistent with v3 invariants after edits.

Full pytest: 536 passed, 1 skipped, 8 e2e deselected (+2 from the new D13 lint test). Mocha test:unit: 375 passing, 2 failing (both pre-existing baseline failures: configEditor-foundation panel-lifecycle + notificationsSection rendering — verified baseline failures pre-existed). tsc --noEmit: clean. Layer 3 Playwright: 5/5 passing against v3 fixtures (Complete bucket label, N/N Complete annotation, force-closed buckets to Complete with [FORCED] badge under v3 force-promotes-all semantic).

Round A verification: gpt-5-4 via ai_router.route(task_type='session-verification'). Focused slice across 5 files (~770 LOC across 8 ranges per memory feedback_split_large_verification_bundles): progress.py read_progress wrapper, gate_checks.py 3 migrated gates, fileSystem.ts isMidSetComplete + readSessionSets state-file block + readClosedSessionsFromLedger, sessionState.ts buildSessions + notStartedPayload + backfillPayload. Cost: $0.28155, elapsed 543s, in=16644 out=15996 tokens, no escalation. Verifier returned REJECTED with 1 must-fix issue: backfillPayload no-plan promotion can emit reader-invalid {status: complete, no sessions[]} snapshots when spec.md lacks totalSessions.

Applied Round A fix: backfillPayload (both TS and Python) now falls through to the not-started shape when buildSessions returns undefined (totalSessions unknown). Prevents emitting {status: complete, no sessions[]} or {status: in-progress, no sessions[]} snapshots that readProgress would reject. Operator intent is preserved via file-presence signals; next boundary write with a known plan re-promotes. Added 2 Python regression tests in test_session_state_v3.py + 1 TS regression test in fileSystem.test.ts. Updated 2 test_read_status.py fixtures to include spec totalSessions (so they exercise the escalation path rather than the new not-started fallback).

Re-ran full suite after Round A fix. pytest: 538 passed (+2 regression tests + 2 read_status fixture updates pass), 1 skipped. Mocha test:unit: 376 passing (+1 regression test), 2 failing (same pre-existing baseline). tsc --noEmit: clean. All Session 3 deltas regression-tested.

Round B verification: gpt-5-4 via ai_router.route(task_type='session-verification'). Same slice strategy as Round A; updated line ranges. Cost: $0.23331, elapsed 187s, in=15030 out=13049 tokens, no escalation. Verifier confirmed all 4 Round A scope items VERIFIED (the read_progress wrapper, gate migration, fileSystem.ts migration, sessionState.ts writer fix). Round B introduced 1 NEW finding: synthesize_v3_from_v2's strict-int filter silently drops malformed legacy values rather than raising. Deliberately deferred — this is a Session 1 design decision the Session 1 verifier explicitly approved ('Pre-filter currentSession and completedSessions[] to strict positive ints'). The filter is defensive (don't crash readers on garbled v2 input) vs strict (raise on garbage). Per memory feedback_verifier_spiral_recruit_codex, Session 3 closes out here rather than spiraling on a strict-vs-defensive philosophy debate.

Registered Session 4 start via python -m ai_router.start_session. Snapshot now currentSession=4, completedSessions=[1,2,3]; sessions[].3 flipped to in-progress (sessions 1-2 stayed complete from Session 1+2; session 3 flipped complete via Session 3 close). work_started event #4 appended to session-events.jsonl.

Created ai_router/migrate_session_state.py (~770 LOC). Inferential v2->v3 migration core: _resolve_total (picks max of legacy total / spec.md / completedSessions / currentSession), _build_v3_sessions (force-promote-all on closed signal else trust completedSessions[]), _strict_positive_int (rejects bool/float/non-int even when ==int), _derive_legacy_triple (v3 -> dual-write derivation), _migrate_state_dict (pure function; runs validate_invariants before any write), migrate_one_set (public entry; returns structured MigrationResult for the CLI and Session 5 in-extension lazy migrator to consume), migrate_all + discover_session_sets. CLI argparse: --scan / --in-place / --strategy {regex,generic,ai,interactive} / --only / --verbose / --json. Atomic write via _atomic_write_json.

Created ai_router/tests/test_migrate_session_state.py with 38 initial cases covering idempotency (v3 skip), closed sets (lifecycle + currentSession>=total branches; spec.md-widened totals; missing completedSessions[]; with full array), in-flight + between-sessions + not-started, cancelled (top status preserved, sessions reflect actual completion), status aliases done/completed -> complete, strict bool/float filtering, title resolution (regex/generic/missing-spec/partial-spec), malformed inputs (no state, bad JSON, top-level array, no totalSessions signal), dry-run vs in-place atomicity + tmpfile cleanup, AI strategy NotImplementedError, unknown-strategy ValueError, discovery sort + filter, CLI exit codes + JSON output + non-TTY interactive fallback. All 38 pass.

Ran python -m ai_router.migrate_session_state --scan docs/session-sets --strategy regex --in-place against the 29 in-repo v2 state files (001-029). 0 violations, 0 malformed; Set 030 itself skipped (already v3 from Session 1). Each migrated file round-trips through read_progress() cleanly. Closed sets without completedSessions[] (007/008/010/011/012/014/etc.) correctly force-promote-all via the lifecycle=closed signal. Set 029 (not-started) becomes all-not-started sessions[]. Committed as its own commit per spec D7 step 2: 0106d97 'Set 030 Session 4: bulk-migrate 29 v2 state files to v3 (dual-write)' so the diff is reviewable independently of the migrator code commit.

D16 Lightweight-tier ergonomics dry-run. Copied dabbler-homehealthcare-accessdb/docs/session-sets/001-forms-detail-uat (4-session closed UAT set, representative Lightweight-tier shape) to C:/tmp/v3-ergonomics-dry-run/. Ran migrator: [migrated] 001-forms-detail-uat (4 complete, 0 in-progress, 0 not-started) - regex titles parsed from spec.md (frmClientDetail UAT (Old #5), frmBudgetServicePlan UAT (Old #6), etc.). Then wrote demo.py walking each transition (start session 1, close session 1, start session 2) with explicit field-by-field diffs; each step round-trips through get_progress() cleanly. Saved to verification-output/lightweight-ergonomics-demo.txt. Finding: D10's 'one field change per transition' holds for mid-set transitions (session-level flip only); set-level status + lifecycleState fields flip at the boundaries (first-ever start and final close).

Authored docs/migration-v3-dry-run.md (~250 lines). Sections: what the migrator does, what it doesn't do, running a dry-run (CLI examples), applying the migration, rolling back (git checkout), inference rules table mapping v2 input shapes to v3 outputs, Lightweight-tier hand-edit ergonomics with worked examples + pointer to the verification-output demo, when to migrate (before vs after GA release), set-ordering note (D15: both orders supported), pre-RC smoke test (this repo's 29-file migration summary), reporting issues, cross-references to schema doc + spec + proposal.

Bumped versions (NOT PUBLISHED per spec D14): dabbler-ai-router 0.3.2 -> 0.4.0rc1 (PEP 440 canonical form for the wheel; also updated ai_router.__version__) and extension 0.13.17 -> 0.14.0-rc.1 (semver). Updated ai_router/CHANGELOG.md + tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/CHANGELOG.md with explicit 'NOT PUBLISHED' notes pointing at Session 5 as the publish point. Built RC artifacts locally: python -m build produces dist/dabbler_ai_router-0.4.0rc1-py3-none-any.whl (includes ai_router/migrate_session_state.py); cd tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration && npx vsce package --no-dependencies produces dabbler-ai-orchestration-0.14.0-rc.1.vsix (728 KB, 20 files). Neither vsce publish nor twine upload invoked.

Full pytest (Layer 1): 568 passed + 1 skipped + 8 e2e deselected. ONE initial fail: test_no_application_reader_accesses_legacy_fields fired against the new migrator's intentional v2-field reads. Fix: added migrate_session_state.py to the file-level D13 allowlist in test_no_legacy_field_reads.py (the migrator IS the v2-compat path; mirrors progress.py and session_state.py allowlist entries). Mocha test:unit (Layer 2): 376 passing, 2 failing (both pre-existing baseline failures unrelated to v3, identical to Session 3's set: configEditor-foundation panel-lifecycle and notificationsSection rendering). Layer 3 Playwright: 5/5 passing in 1.7m total against v3 fixtures (Complete bucket, N/N annotation, [FORCED] badge, in-flight annotation, cancelled bucket).

Round A verification: gpt-5-4 via ai_router.route(task_type='session-verification'). Focused slice of migrate_session_state.py (~470 LOC across 3 ranges per memory feedback_split_large_verification_bundles; bundled the inferential core + public entry points; skipped CLI argparse / output formatting / __all__). Cost: $0.189845, elapsed 163s, in=7442 out=11416 tokens, no escalation. Verifier returned REJECTED with 3 must-fix issues: (1) closed-signal disjunct compares currentSession to resolved total (after spec.md widening) instead of LEGACY totalSessions, causing a v2 file like {status:complete, totalSessions:3, currentSession:3, completedSessions:[], spec.md:4} to fall out of the closed branch and surface as rule-7 WOULD-VIOLATE; (2) future-schema files (schemaVersion>3) not refused — fell through and were re-migrated by v2 inference, silently downgrading; (3) _atomic_write_json uses a fixed .{basename}.tmp path (collision risk on concurrent runs) and leaves the temp file behind if json.dump/write raises mid-write.

Applied all 3 must-fix issues from Round A: (1) _build_v3_sessions now reads legacy_total_int = state.get('totalSessions') gated by _strict_positive_int, and the closed-signal alternative branch compares current_int >= legacy_total_int (not resolved total) — preserves the operator's 'I closed here' signal even when spec.md is later widened; (2) migrate_one_set now refuses schemaVersion > 3 (returns ACTION_SKIPPED_FUTURE_SCHEMA) and refuses v3 files missing sessions[] (returns ACTION_SKIPPED_MALFORMED instead of re-running v2 inference); (3) _atomic_write_json now uses tempfile.mkstemp(prefix='.{basename}.', suffix='.tmp', dir=...) for a unique per-call path + try/finally with best-effort os.unlink on the temp before re-raising on any failure path. Added 6 regression tests in TestRoundAFixes class: test_closed_signal_uses_legacy_total_not_resolved_total, test_closed_signal_no_legacy_total_only_lifecycle_branch_fires, test_future_schema_version_refused, test_v3_without_sessions_is_malformed_not_re_migrated, test_atomic_write_uses_unique_tempfile_and_cleans_up_on_failure, test_atomic_write_two_concurrent_calls_dont_collide. All 44 migrator tests pass.

Re-ran full pytest suite after Round A fixes: 574 passed, 1 skipped, 8 e2e deselected (+6 from new TestRoundAFixes regression tests). No regressions in any pre-existing tests. Mocha + Playwright still green from pre-fix run (Round A fixes are scoped to migrate_session_state.py + its tests; no extension code touched).

Round B verification: gpt-5-4 via ai_router.route(task_type='session-verification'). Same slice strategy as Round A; updated line ranges for the post-fix file. Cost: $0.0676625, elapsed (in=6023, out=3507) tokens, no escalation.
